question:
A nuclear plant is located in an area susceptible to both high winds
and earthquakes. From the historial data, the mean frequency of large earthquakes
capable of damaging important plant structures is one every 50 years. The corresponding frequency of damaging high winds is once in 25 years. During a strong
earthquake, the probability of structure damage is 0.1. During high winds, the damage
probability is 0.05. Assume that earthquakes and high winds can be described by
independent Poisson random variables and that the damages caused by these events
are independent. Let us exercise the following questions:
(a) What is the probability of having strong winds but not large earthquakes during
a 10-year period?
(b) What is the probability of having strong winds and large earthquakes in the
10-year period?
(c) What is the probability of building damage during the 10-year period?
answer:
To begin with question (a), let the random variable $X$ and $Y$ represent the number of earthquakes and the number of occurrences of high winds, respectively. We assume that the two random variables are statistically independent. The mean of $X$ and $Y$ are, respectively, given by

$$
\lambda_X=\frac{1}{50 \text { years }}(10 \text { years })=0.2
$$

and

$$
\lambda_Y=\frac{1}{25 \text { year }}(10 \text { years })=0.4
$$


The conditional damage probabilities are given as follows:

$$
P(\text { damage/earthquake })=0.1
$$

And
$$
P(\text { damage } / \text { wind })=0.05
$$


Let the event
$A=\{$ strong winds and no earthquakes $\}$.
$B=\{$ strong winds and large earthquakes $\}$.
$C=\{$ building damage $\}$.
Assuming that winds and earthquakes are independents, the probability of having strong winds but not earthquakes during the 10 -year period can be written as

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(A) & =P(w \text { inds }) P(\text { no earthquakes }) \\
& =[1-P(\text { no winds })] P(\text { no earthquakes })
\end{aligned}
$$


Therefore, we obtain

$$
P(A)=\left(1-e^{-0.4}\right)\left(e^{-0.2}\right)=0.27
$$
(b) The probability of having strong winds and earthquakes during the 10-year period can be obtained

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(B) & =P(\text { winds }) P(\text { earthquakes }) \\
& =[1-P(\text { no winds })][1-P(\text { no earthquakes })] \\
& =\left(1-e^{-0.4}\right)\left(1-e^{-0.2}\right)=0.06
\end{aligned}
$$

(c) We assume that there will not be multiple occurrences of earthquakes and high winds during the 10-year period. Therefore, the probability of building damage can be written as

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(C) & =P(\text { damage/earthquakes }) P(\text { earthquakes }) \\
& +\mathrm{P}(\text { damage/wind }) \mathrm{P}(\text { wind }) \\
& -\mathrm{P}(\text { damage/earthquakes and wind }) \mathrm{P}(\text { earthquake and wind }) \\
& =P(\text { damage/earthquakes }) P(\text { earthquakes }) \\
& +\mathrm{P}(\text { damage/wind }) \mathrm{P}(\text { wind }) \\
& -\mathrm{P}(\text { damage/earthquakes }) \mathrm{P}(\text { damage/wind }) \mathrm{P}(\text { earthquake and wind }) \\
& =\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-0.2}\right)(0.1)+\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-0.4}\right)(0.05)-(0.05)(0.1)(0.06) \\
& =0.0343
\end{aligned}
$$
